conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're asking about.

Let me start by asking: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 15 years old and lives with me here at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have a 15-year-old son named Alex who lives with you at home.

To assess your eligibility properly, I need to gather some more details. Let me start with:

**Are you responsible for Alex? This means do you provide for his daily care and needs (such as food, clothing, housing, etc.)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for Alex - I take care of all his daily needs, provide his food, clothing, and everything he needs at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. Now I need to understand a bit more about your situation:

**Are you or your partner currently living or working in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're based in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Now I need to ask about Alex's education or training status, as this is important for children over 16 (though Alex is 15, it's good to understand his situation):

**Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training? For example, is he attending secondary school?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's still at school - he's in Year 10 at secondary school doing his GCSEs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for that information. I have a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Does Alex live with anyone else who might also be responsible for him, such as another parent or partner?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, it's just me and Alex. He lives with me full-time, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Just a couple more questions to ensure I have all the information I need:

**Is Alex currently in any of these situations: in hospital for more than 12 weeks, in local authority care, or in custody?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply. He's not in hospital, not in care, and not in custody. He's just at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

**Are you receiving certain benefits such as Universal Credit, Income Support, income-based Jobseeker's Allowance, or Pension Credit? Or does your individual income (or your partner's if you have one) exceed £50,000 per year?**

(This is to understand if the High Income Child Benefit Charge might apply to you, though it doesn't affect your eligibility to claim.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not receiving any of those benefits, and my income is below £50,000 a year.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me review the eligibility rules to make a final assessment for you.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on all the information you've provided and the official UK government rules, I can now provide you with a decision on your eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on your circumstances, you meet all the requirements:

✓ Alex is under 16 years old (he's 15)
✓ You are responsible for him and provide for his daily needs
✓ He lives with you full-time in the UK
✓ He's in approved education (secondary school)
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
✓ He's not in hospital, care, or custody

**What happens next:**
You can go ahead and apply for Child Benefit. Since your income is below £50,000 per year, you won't face the High Income Child Benefit Charge, which means you can receive the full benefit without any tax implications.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about claiming Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: 1) He is under 16 years old (currently 15), which means you normally qualify. 2) You are responsible for him - you provide for his daily care and needs including food, clothing, and housing. 3) He lives with you full-time in the UK (Manchester). 4) He is attending secondary school (Year 10, doing GCSEs), which is approved education. 5) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. 6) He is not in hospital for more than 12 weeks, not in local authority care, and not in custody. According to the official guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All these conditions are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. They meet all the core requirements: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live together in the UK, no one else is claiming for the child, and the child is not in any excluded situations (hospital/care/custody). The claimant's income is below £50,000 so they would not face the High Income Child Benefit Charge.

The claimant's income is below £50,000 so they would not face the High Income Child Benefit Charge.
